bullets.text: Ukrainian President Zelensky is in Downing Street for war talks with European leaders, as the US President's attention shifts towards the war with Iran.
US health officials warn that the spread of Ebola in central Africa could potentially rival the record outbreak seen in 2014, with modelling indicating a dangerous trajectory.
Israel has conducted strikes in a Beirut suburb, stating these attacks were a response to Hezbollah's firing into Israeli territory.

Remote Indigenous communities in Australia are experiencing worsening life expectancy rates, with one Northern Territory community calling for an investigation after 15 preventable deaths in six months.
Australia is facing its largest outbreak of diphtheria in living memory, a disease previously almost eradicated, which is now spreading in remote Indigenous communities.
Professor Richard Scolyer, a world-renowned cancer researcher and former Australian of the Year, has died at 59 after being diagnosed with inoperable brain cancer in 2023.
Major airlines are addressing increased demand for Asian travel, consolidation within the industry, and the impact of rising fuel prices.
Bond traders anticipate a significant surge in consumer prices this week, which could intensify pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
A potential influx of new shares from companies seeking to fund artificial intelligence initiatives is raising concerns on Wall Street about market capacity and buyer demand.
